text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Content:
SUMMARY OF THE FIVE CRITERIA (A-E) USED TO EVALUATE IF A TAXON BELONGS IN AN IUCN RED LIST THREATENED CATEGORY (CRITICALLY ENDANGERED, ENDANGERED OR VULNERABLE). 1 A. Population size reduction. Population reduction (measured over the longer of 10 years or 3 generations) based on any of A1 to A4
raw_text: SUMMARY OF THE FIVE CRITERIA (A-E) USED TO EVALUATE IF A TAXON BELONGS IN AN IUCN RED LIST THREATENED CATEGORY (CRITICALLY ENDANGERED, ENDANGERED OR VULNERABLE). 1 A. Population size reduction. Population reduction (measured over the longer of 10 years or 3 generations) based on any of A1 to A4

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: A2, A3 & A4
Content:
≥ 80% ≥ 50% ≥ 30% (a) direct observation [except A3] (b) an index of abundance appropriate to the taxon (c) a decline in area of occupancy (AOO), extent of occurrence (EOO) and/or habitat quality (d) actual or potential levels of exploitation (e) effects of introduced taxa, hybridization, pathogens, pollutants, competitors or parasites. A1 Population reduction observed, estimated, inferred, or suspected in the past where the causes of the reduction are clearly reversible AND understood AND have ceased. A2 Population reduction observed, estimated, inferred, or suspected in the past where the causes of reduction may not have ceased OR may not be understood OR may not be reversible. A3 Population reduction projected, inferred or suspected to be met in the future (up to a maximum of 100 years) [(a) cannot be used for A3]. A4 An observed, estimated, inferred, projected or suspected population reduction where the time period must include both the past and the future (up to a max. of 100 years in future), and where the causes of reduction may not have ceased OR may not be understood OR may not be reversible. based on any of the following:
raw_text: ≥ 80% ≥ 50% ≥ 30% (a) direct observation [except A3] (b) an index of abundance appropriate to the taxon (c) a decline in area of occupancy (AOO), extent of occurrence (EOO) and/or habitat quality (d) actual or potential levels of exploitation (e) effects of introduced taxa, hybridization, pathogens, pollutants, competitors or parasites. A1 Population reduction observed, estimated, inferred, or suspected in the past where the causes of the reduction are clearly reversible AND understood AND have ceased. A2 Population reduction observed, estimated, inferred, or suspected in the past where the causes of reduction may not have ceased OR may not be understood OR may not be reversible. A3 Population reduction projected, inferred or suspected to be met in the future (up to a maximum of 100 years) [(a) cannot be used for A3]. A4 An observed, estimated, inferred, projected or suspected population reduction where the time period must include both the past and the future (up to a max. of 100 years in future), and where the causes of reduction may not have ceased OR may not be understood OR may not be reversible. based on any of the following:

Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: A2, A3 & A4
Content:
B. Geographic range in the form of either B1 (extent of occurrence) AND/OR B2 (area of occupancy)
raw_text: B. Geographic range in the form of either B1 (extent of occurrence) AND/OR B2 (area of occupancy)

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: ≤ 10 (a) Severely fragmented OR Number of locations
Content:
= 1 ≤ 5 (b) Continuing decline observed, estimated, inferred or projected in any of: (i) extent of occurrence; (ii) area of occupancy; (iii) area, extent and/or quality of habitat; (iv) number of locations or subpopulations; (v) number of mature individuals (c) Extreme fluctuations in any of: (i) extent of occurrence; (ii) area of occupancy; (iii) number of locations or subpopulations; (iv) number of mature individuals
raw_text: = 1 ≤ 5 (b) Continuing decline observed, estimated, inferred or projected in any of: (i) extent of occurrence; (ii) area of occupancy; (iii) area, extent and/or quality of habitat; (iv) number of locations or subpopulations; (v) number of mature individuals (c) Extreme fluctuations in any of: (i) extent of occurrence; (ii) area of occupancy; (iii) number of locations or subpopulations; (iv) number of mature individuals
section_title: ≤ 10 (a) Severely fragmented OR Number of locations
section_path: ≤ 10 (a) Severely fragmented OR Number of locations

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: AND at least one of C1 or C2
Content:
20% in 5 years or 2 generations (whichever is longer) 10% in 10 years or 3 generations (whichever is longer) 25% in 3 years or 1 generation (whichever is longer) C1. An observed, estimated or projected continuing decline of at least (up to a max. of 100 years in future): C2. An observed, estimated, projected or inferred continuing decline AND at least 1 of the following 3 conditions:
raw_text: 20% in 5 years or 2 generations (whichever is longer) 10% in 10 years or 3 generations (whichever is longer) 25% in 3 years or 1 generation (whichever is longer) C1. An observed, estimated or projected continuing decline of at least (up to a max. of 100 years in future): C2. An observed, estimated, projected or inferred continuing decline AND at least 1 of the following 3 conditions:
section_title: AND at least one of C1 or C2
section_path: AND at least one of C1 or C2

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: < 50 < 250 D1. < 1,000
Content:
D2. Only applies to the VU category Restricted area of occupancy or number of locations with a plausible future threat that could drive the taxon to CR or EX in a very short time.
raw_text: D2. Only applies to the VU category Restricted area of occupancy or number of locations with a plausible future threat that could drive the taxon to CR or EX in a very short time.

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: Critically Endangered Endangered Vulnerable
Content:
≥ 50% in 10 years or 3 generations, whichever is longer (100 years max.) ≥ 20% in 20 years or 5 generations, whichever is longer (100 years max.)
raw_text: ≥ 50% in 10 years or 3 generations, whichever is longer (100 years max.) ≥ 20% in 20 years or 5 generations, whichever is longer (100 years max.)

section_path: Critically Endangered Endangered Vulnerable

text:
Source: RL_criteria_summary_sheet.pdf
Page: 1
Block type: text
Section path: Indicating the probability of extinction in the wild to be:
Content:
≥ 10% in 100 years 1 Use of this summary sheet requires full understanding of the IUCN Red List Categories and Criteria and Guidelines for Using the IUCN Red List Categories and Criteria . Please refer to both documents for explanations of terms and concepts used here.
